user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] System detected Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected for account Tailspin Toys. Generating output.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-16 11:55 EST
Subject: URGENT: QBR preparation Issue at Tailspin Toys

Just checking in on the QBR preparation proposal. Our VP Engineering is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
Date: 2024-02-08 10:17 EST
Subject: Re: QBR preparation Discussion - Next Steps

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with QBR preparation. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-28 13:29 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Tailspin Toys QBR preparation

Just checking in on the QBR preparation proposal. Our VP Engineering is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: QBR preparation Discussion - Tailspin Toys
Date: 2024-01-21
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys), Raj Mehta (Microsoft), Michelle (Tailspin Toys)

[00:02:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on QBR preparation from our last session.

[00:08:00] Michelle (Tailspin Toys): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

[00:12:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): The timeline for security compliance is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:16:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): The timeline for vendor dependencies is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:21:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:26:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

[00:30:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:34:00] Michelle (Tailspin Toys): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is budget approval.

[00:36:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:38:00] Michelle (Tailspin Toys): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

Score 1: Evidence fabricated or significantly misattributed
